projectName: Stock Prediction Platform — Production Audit Fixes
description: Fix all issues identified in the 2026-04-03 full production audit. Covers: API port mismatch breaking all frontend tabs, Flink job failures (sentiment-stream FAILED, feast-writer/indicator-stream stuck CANCELLING), daily-feature-store cron 2x consecutive failures, Strimzi/MinIO/Redis restart loops, Analytics UI bugs, and KServe predictor instability.

userStories.title: Fix API port mismatch — point VITE_API_URL to localhost:8010
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Vite dev server to reach the API so that all frontend tabs load data instead of showing ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED.
acceptanceCriteria: Change VITE_API_URL in stock-prediction-platform/services/frontend/.env.local from http://localhost:8000 to http://localhost:8010
Add a Vite proxy in vite.config.ts: proxy '/api' → 'http://localhost:8010' (so both direct and proxied calls work)
Navigate to http://localhost:5173/dashboard in browser using dev-browser skill and confirm live data loads (treemap visible, no ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED in console)
Navigate to http://localhost:5173/forecasts and confirm forecast table loads
Navigate to http://localhost:5173/drift and confirm drift data loads
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fixed VITE_API_URL from 8000→8010, added Vite proxy for /api, fixed CORS middleware order in FastAPI (CORSMiddleware must be added last to be outermost), rebuilt and redeployed API. Dashboard treemap visible with live data, Drift loads with 15 events. Forecasts failure is pre-existing (no trained model — unrelated to port fix).

userStories.title: Diagnose and fix sentiment-stream Flink job Python crash (exit code 1)
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need the sentiment-stream Flink job to start successfully so that the sentiment data pipeline is operational.
acceptanceCriteria: Run: kubectl logs -n flink -l app=sentiment-stream --previous --tail=100 to get the crash traceback
Identify the root cause of 'Python process exits with code: 1' from the sentiment_stream.py
Fix the Python error in the relevant source file under stock-prediction-platform/
Rebuild the Docker image in Minikube context: eval $(minikube docker-env) && docker build -t stock-flink-sentiment-stream:latest -f <Dockerfile> .
Restart the FlinkDeployment: kubectl delete flinkdeployment sentiment-stream -n flink && kubectl apply -f k8s/flink/ (or relevant manifest)
Verify: kubectl get flinkdeployments sentiment-stream -n flink shows RUNNING/STABLE within 60s
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Root cause: three bugs in sentiment_stream.py — (1) ARRAY_LENGTH() is not a valid Flink SQL function (use CARDINALITY()), (2) scan.startup.mode='group-offsets' fails when consumer group has no stored offsets (changed to 'latest-offset'), (3) manifest used minio-secrets (MINIO_ROOT_USER/PASSWORD) instead of minio-s3-credentials (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID/SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) which the S3 plugin requires. Fixed all three, rebuilt image in Minikube context, redeployed. Verified RUNNING/STABLE.

userStories.title: Investigate daily-feature-store cron failures and fix root cause
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need the daily-feature-store cron job to succeed so that Feast feature views are populated and Analytics Feature Freshness shows real data.
acceptanceCriteria: Run: kubectl logs -n ml -l job-name=daily-feature-store-29585880 --tail=100 to get the failure logs
Also check: kubectl describe job daily-feature-store-29585880 -n ml for pod events
Identify root cause (OOMKill, missing secret, Python error, DB connection, etc.)
Fix the underlying issue in the relevant manifest, script, or source file
Manually trigger a feature-store job: kubectl create job manual-feature-store-fix --from=cronjob/daily-feature-store -n ml
Verify the manually triggered job completes: kubectl wait --for=condition=complete job/manual-feature-store-fix -n ml --timeout=300s
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Restart feast-writer and indicator-stream Flink jobs stuck in CANCELLING/UPGRADING
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need feast-writer and indicator-stream Flink jobs to be RUNNING so that feature data flows from Kafka to Feast and processed-features topic is populated.
acceptanceCriteria: Force restart feast-writer: kubectl patch flinkdeployment feast-writer -n flink --type=merge -p '{"spec":{"restartNonce":1}}' OR delete and reapply its manifest
Force restart indicator-stream: same approach
Verify: kubectl get flinkdeployments -n flink shows both feast-writer and indicator-stream with state RUNNING or STABLE within 120s
Check feast-writer logs no longer show 'Node 0 disconnected' every 5 minutes (at least 10 minutes clean)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix Strimzi cluster operator restart loop — add resource limits and investigate OOMKill
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need the strimzi-cluster-operator to be stable so that Kafka entity-operator stops restarting and the Kafka cluster stays healthy.
acceptanceCriteria: Check if OOMKill is the cause: kubectl describe pod -n storage -l name=strimzi-cluster-operator | grep -A10 'Last State'
Check current resource requests/limits on the strimzi-cluster-operator deployment
Patch or edit the strimzi-cluster-operator deployment to set memory limit >= 512Mi (or 768Mi if currently lower)
If operator is managed by a Helm release or manifest in k8s/, update the relevant file too
Verify restart count stabilizes: kubectl get pod -n storage -l name=strimzi-cluster-operator --watch for 2 minutes shows no new restarts
Verify kafka-entity-operator also stops restarting as a consequence
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix MinIO restart loop — add resource limits and PVC health check
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need MinIO to be stable so that Flink checkpoints can be written reliably to S3-compatible storage.
acceptanceCriteria: Check OOMKill: kubectl describe pod -n storage -l app=minio | grep -A10 'Last State'
Check current resource requests/limits on the minio deployment
Set memory limit >= 512Mi and request >= 256Mi on the minio deployment in k8s/storage/ manifest
Apply the change: kubectl apply -f k8s/storage/minio.yaml (or equivalent)
Verify minio pod restarts reduce: kubectl get pod -n storage -l app=minio for 2 minutes shows stable
Verify MinIO is accessible: kubectl exec -n storage deployment/minio -- mc ls minio/ (or similar health check)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Stabilize Redis — add maxmemory-policy and resource limits
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need Redis to stop restarting (39 restarts) so that API response caching is reliable.
acceptanceCriteria: Check restart reason: kubectl describe pod -n storage -l app=redis | grep -A5 'Last State'
Update the Redis deployment or ConfigMap in k8s/storage/ to add: --maxmemory 256mb --maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru
Set memory limit >= 300Mi and request >= 128Mi on the redis deployment
Apply changes: kubectl apply -f k8s/storage/redis.yaml (or equivalent)
Verify pod is stable for 2 minutes: no new OOMKill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Investigate KServe stock-model-serving-predictor instability (12 restarts)
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need the KServe inference predictor pod to be stable so that model serving is reliable for production forecasts.
acceptanceCriteria: Check restart cause: kubectl describe pod -n ml -l serving.kserve.io/inferenceservice=stock-model-serving | grep -A10 'Last State'
Check predictor logs for Python/OOM errors: kubectl logs -n ml -l serving.kserve.io/inferenceservice=stock-model-serving --tail=50
If OOMKill: update InferenceService resource limits in k8s/ml/ to set memory >= 1Gi
If startup probe failing: adjust initialDelaySeconds or failureThreshold in the InferenceService manifest
Apply the fix and verify predictor pods reach Running state without restarting
Test inference: curl -s http://$(minikube ip):$(kubectl get svc -n ml stock-model-serving-predictor -o jsonpath='{.spec.ports[0].nodePort}')/v2/health/ready or equivalent
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix Analytics OLAP Candle Chart — ResponsiveContainer renders at -1x-1px
userStories.description: As a user, I want the OLAP Candle Chart on the Analytics page to render correctly so that I can see price candles for any ticker.
acceptanceCriteria: Find the OLAPCandleChart component in stock-prediction-platform/services/frontend/src/
Wrap the ResponsiveContainer in a div with explicit min-height (e.g. className='min-h-[240px]' or style={{minHeight: 240}})
Alternatively add height prop directly to ResponsiveContainer: height={240}
Verify no more 'width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' console warnings
Navigate to http://localhost:5173/analytics using dev-browser skill and confirm the OLAP chart container is visible and has correct dimensions
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Wire Analytics stat cards — Argo CD Sync, Feast Latency p99, CA Last Refresh
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Analytics page stat cards to show real values instead of N/A so that I have a complete view of platform health.
acceptanceCriteria: Find the Analytics page component and identify which API endpoints these 3 cards call
Check if the backend endpoints exist: GET /analytics/summary — verify it returns argocd_sync_status, feast_latency_p99, ca_last_refresh fields
If endpoints are missing data: add the fields to the /analytics/summary FastAPI router in services/api/app/routers/analytics.py (query Prometheus or return static healthy/N/A based on actual checks)
If endpoints exist but frontend mapping is wrong: fix the field mapping in the Analytics component
After fix, verify: curl http://localhost:8010/analytics/summary returns all 3 fields with non-null values
Navigate to http://localhost:5173/analytics using dev-browser skill and confirm cards show values other than N/A
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Start sentiment-writer Flink job — investigate CREATED/STABLE and deploy
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need the sentiment-writer Flink job to transition from CREATED to RUNNING so that aggregated sentiment data is written to the feature store.
acceptanceCriteria: Check the FlinkDeployment spec for sentiment-writer: kubectl get flinkdeployment sentiment-writer -n flink -o yaml
Verify the job spec has state: running (not suspended)
If state is 'suspended': patch it to running: kubectl patch flinkdeployment sentiment-writer -n flink --type=merge -p '{"spec":{"job":{"state":"running"}}}'
Verify sentiment-writer transitions to RUNNING: kubectl get flinkdeployments -n flink sentiment-writer within 60s
Check logs for any startup errors: kubectl logs -n flink deployment/sentiment-writer --tail=30
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Monitor daily-drift first execution and verify drift data populates
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need to verify the daily-drift cron job (created 4h ago, never run) executes successfully at 22:00 so that the Drift page shows real drift events.
acceptanceCriteria: Manually trigger the daily-drift job to test it now (don't wait for 22:00): kubectl create job manual-daily-drift-test --from=cronjob/daily-drift -n ml
Wait for completion: kubectl wait --for=condition=complete job/manual-daily-drift-test -n ml --timeout=600s
Check job logs: kubectl logs -n ml -l job-name=manual-daily-drift-test --tail=50

Verify drift records exist in DB: kubectl exec -n ingestion deployment/stock-api -- python -c "from app.database import engine; from sqlalchemy import text; print(engine.connect().execute(text('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM drift_events')).fetchone())" OR via curl http://localhost:8010/models/drift
Navigate to http://localhost:5173/drift using dev-browser skill and confirm drift data loads
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Investigate test-linear ML job failure and clean up
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need to understand if the test-linear ML job failure (3d ago) impacts production model training or is an orphaned test artifact.
acceptanceCriteria: Get test-linear job logs: kubectl logs -n ml -l job-name=test-linear --tail=50
Determine if it was a one-off test job or a recurring job that impacts prod
If it was an ad-hoc test job with no production impact: delete it to clean up: kubectl delete job test-linear -n ml
Verify no recurring CronJob named test-linear exists: kubectl get cronjobs -n ml | grep test-linear
Document finding in progress.txt
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix MUI LoadingButton deprecation in Backtest page
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Backtest page to use the current MUI API so that there are no deprecation console warnings.
acceptanceCriteria: Find the Backtest page component using LoadingButton from @mui/lab
Replace <LoadingButton loading={isLoading} ...> with <Button loading={isLoading} ...> from @mui/material
Ensure loading prop behavior is identical (shows spinner during async operation)
No console warnings about LoadingButton after the change
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Post-fix integration verification — all tabs healthy end-to-end
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want to verify that all frontend tabs load successfully and all critical backend services are healthy after applying all fixes.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigate to http://localhost:5173/dashboard — confirm treemap and market data load (no ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED)
Navigate to http://localhost:5173/forecasts — confirm forecast table shows data for at least one horizon
Navigate to http://localhost:5173/drift — confirm drift events or metrics load
Navigate to http://localhost:5173/analytics — confirm Stream Health shows RUNNING jobs, Feature Freshness shows non-null values
Navigate to http://localhost:5173/models — confirm model table and SHAP charts still render
Run: kubectl get flinkdeployments -n flink — all jobs should show RUNNING or STABLE (no FAILED or stuck CANCELLING)
Run: kubectl get pods -n storage — strimzi-cluster-operator and minio should have no new restarts since fix
Run: curl http://localhost:8010/health — returns status: ok
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
